com.mentor.chs.api.workbook.data

Interface IXTableData

  • All Superinterfaces:
    IXWorkbookData

    @NotToBeImplemented
public interface IXTableData
extends IXWorkbookData
    Data object used to present a table in Capital Workbooks. The user may return tabular data, here is an example provider: 

     
 public class BOMTable implements IXWorkbookProvider<IXTableData>
 {
 	public IXTableData generate(IXSubAssemblyWorkbookContext context) throws ProviderException
 	{
 		IXTableData table = context.getProviderDataFactory().createTable();		// use the factory to create the table
 		table.setColumnCount(3);											// it is necessary to tell the number of columns
 		table.addRow(createTableHeader(table));									// first add a row which will be a header
 		table.addRows(createTableRows(context, table));							// then add the actual data rows
 		return table;
 	}

 	private IXCellData[] createTableHeader(IXTableData table)
 	{
 		IXCellData[] header = new IXCellData[3];

 		header[0] = table.createCell();					// Unlike tables, cells are not created from the factory
 		header[0].setValue("Sub-assembly");

 		header[1] = table.createCell();
 		header[1].setValue("Part No.");

 		header[2] = table.createCell();
 		header[2].setValue("Cost");
 		return header;
 	}

 	private List<IXCellData[]> createTableRows(IXSubAssemblyWorkbookContext context, IXTableData table)
 	{
 		Collection<? extends IXSubAssembly> children = context.getCurrentSubAssembly().getChildren();
 		List<IXCellData[]> rows = new ArrayList<IXCellData[]>();
 		for (IXSubAssembly child : children) {
 			rows.add(createTableRow(child, table));
 		}
 		return rows;
 	}

 	private IXCellData[] createTableRow(IXSubAssembly child, IXTableData table)
 	{
 		IXCellData[] row = new IXCellData[3];

 		row[0] = table.createCell();
 		row[0].setValue(child.getAttribute(IXAttributes.Name));

 		row[1] = table.createCell();
 		row[1].setValue(child.getAttribute(IXAttributes.PartNumber));

 		row[2] = table.createCell();
 		row[2].setValue(child.getAttribute(IXAttributes.CostResultValue));

 		return row;
 	}

 	public Class<? extends IXTableData> getGenerateDataType()
 	{
 		return IXTableData.class;
 	}
 }
    Note: There is no validation on the data so a row with more column values that the table will not be printed.

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Instance Methods Abstract Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      void addRow(java.util.Collection<? extends IXCellData> row)
      Adds a row to the table.
      void addRows(java.util.Collection<? extends java.util.Collection<? extends IXCellData>> rows)
      Adds a list of rows to the table rows are appended to the tail of the current available rows
      void setColumnCount(int columns)
      sets the number of columns for this table
      void setNumberOfHeaderRows(int headers)
      Set the number of header rows, header rows are assumed to be the top rows

    • Method Detail

      • setColumnCount

        void setColumnCount(int columns)
        sets the number of columns for this table
        Parameters:
        columns - number of columns

      • setNumberOfHeaderRows

        void setNumberOfHeaderRows(int headers)
        Set the number of header rows, header rows are assumed to be the top rows
        Parameters:
        headers - number of header rows

      • addRow

        void addRow(java.util.Collection<? extends IXCellData> row)
        Adds a row to the table. Rows are added in order.

        The number of items in the collection should match the column count accounting for spanning. For instance if a table has 3 columns then this collection could contain:

        - 3 IXCellData where each cell data spans 1 column

        2 IXCellData where 1 cell data spans 1 coulmn and the other spans 2 columns

        or 1 IXCellData where the cell data spans 3 columns.

        Parameters:
        row - a row to be added

      • addRows

        void addRows(java.util.Collection<? extends java.util.Collection<? extends IXCellData>> rows)
        Adds a list of rows to the table rows are appended to the tail of the current available rows
        Parameters:
        rows - rows to be added
